input_text:
Categories: Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Burgers, Fish & Chips, Sandwiches | Review: Great ambiance and yummy food. Fair prices and great portions. 
I had dinner and lunch there and the fish, clam chowder were delicious. 
Friendly staff too.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Tiki Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: Kind of underwhelming. Very awkward bar arrangement and very tight layout. Not a comfortable place to be for very long. I liked the Mai Tai though.

Categories: Bakeries, Food Trucks, Food, Cupcakes | Review: The best cupcakes I have ever had is from Enjoy Cupcakes in the Santa Barbara Public Market downtown. They have mini cupcakes for $2 each and you can get one of each flavor (6 flavors) for $10 that comes in a cute little mini egg carton. Enjoy Cupcakes never gets boring because they change their flavors often and I have yet to try a single one that is a miss. From the Chocolate Malt sprinkled with tiny bits of In 'n Out fries to Chardonnay and the Neapolitan, the flavors can never go wrong. The problem with many cupcakes is that they're too dry and they crumble at the touch. But Enjoy Cupcakes are always perfectly baked and the gooey fillings on the inside keep it moist and scrumptious. The location is also perfect; the Public Market is a hip little indoor place with a few other good eateries like the Thai restaurant and the unique ice cream shop that I have yet to try.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Venues & Event Spaces, Peruvian, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Nightlife, Latin American, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Tapas/Small Plates, Mexican | Review: Food was extremely greasy: specifically the quesadilla de pork belly. There were puddles of grease on every dish we ordered. The garnish overpowered the "tapas" feel that the dishes should've had. 

Drinks were decent, ordered a Cadillac margarita which compared to those of "Mexican" chain restaurants. 

Service was great and so was the decor, probably the only reason I would return. 

Lastly; why does this place say "Mexican" when there's nothing "Mexican" to their dishes....

Well, the guacamole and chips were as authentic as "Mexican" can be.

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife, Barbeque, Sandwiches | Review: For the price, I honestly don't think it's worth it. I have eaten here twice and each time left something to be desired. I have tried the sampler plate with chicken, brisket, and sausage. The chicken was greasy and not as hot as I would expect freshly cooked chicken would be. The brisket had a similar problem, but more so I hoped it would be more tender. The sausage was very good, but really for a link of sausage to be that expensive is ridiculous. The bread was disgusting, as I believe other reviews have mentioned. BBQ sauces were all awesome though. 
On my second trip (for lunch) I tried the pulled pork sandwich. The flavors were good as was the meat, but the bun was way overpowering and a bland texture that absorbed some of the wonderful seasoning.
For sides I have tried the mac and cheese (deliciously decadent), the greens (wayyy too salty), and the coleslaw (nothing to write home about).
As for service, everyone is polite, yet I don't feel that they are fully engaged and more trying to "perform". Maybe it's just me, but unless I am involved in an important conversation I enjoy feeling a camaraderie with the folks serving. Instead it just felt like some superficial upsell instead of honest insight.
As this place has only been open for a couple months, I hope they will work out some of their kinks. I would honestly hope it becomes understood that while this is upscale for a barbeque restaurant it is not of the same caliber of Julienne, despite the same owners. The price range should not be the same.

Categories: Restaurants, Nightlife, Mexican, Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: All I can say is the the view of the beach and the amazing food they make... is delicious.  Heat service as well. I was blown away. I'm usually picky about this food type but it surprised me on how flavorful it was. Not to mention... if there's a bit of a wait. You can always walk around the corner and grab a drink.

Categories: Wine Bars, Bars, Beer Bar, Nightlife | Review: I was so sad that they were not serving frozé! :( I saw all the Yelp pictures and drove out a little further just to have one! I'm sad there was no therapy for me! :/ 
Lol so even though we weren't hungry, we figured we eat something and drink whatever since we were already here. 
I ordered the peanut butter bacon jelly something burger, something like that ask about it. I really enjoyed the kale salad that came along with it. I had some vino and ordered a few rounds. Service was great, place was clean and the vibe is good. 
Sadly, it wasn't frozé all day for me.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Who gives a donkey shit that some old bag who loved terrines and ugly dresses thought this was the best Mexican food around. 

And honestly the line of food tourists and middle age SB locals outside is quite obscene. Go back to your Costco cookies, frappuccinos and lobster bisque.

Anywho--the handmade tortillas are the kind of foodstuff that people dream about, and each taco creation is spectacularly fresh and unique.  This is unlike any "tacqueria" I have ever been to.  

Note--No burritos.  Know what you are going to order before getting to the window inside.  Once inside the menu is directly to the right, and then you only have a minute or so to solidify your choices before ordering.  Don't hold up the line being some hem and haw douche bag.

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: My wife and I both work in the restaurant industry and have become accustomed to a certain level of quality when choosing to dine out. We could not speak higher of the service and food perfection we received from Opal. It is so difficult to celebrate special occasions during quarantine, and they were willing to go above and beyond to help us make our day exceptional. We both ordered the filet, and it was cooked to perfection! The kitchen team was kind enough to plate our meal into special containers, so it did not appear as "to-go food" and instead as we would see it if we were to dine in. Thank you so much for helping us celebrate our one year anniversary! Excellent guest service!! YUM!

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Seafood, French | Review: Was hoping this place was going to be amazing, as it was our big Saturday night dinner in SB. It was solid, but not the meal I had been anticipating. The menu varies, featuring local ingredients, which I think is great...but it was VERY limited. My friend is a pescetarian and she only had one entree option. I opted for the gnocci with beef cheeks, which I assumed would be gnocci with a beef ragu. Instead it was six gnocci in a beef stock with large chunks of beef cheeks. The beef was tasty and tender, but the gnocci were literally flavorless, so that was disappointing. Also, for the prices they charge (which aren't crazy but aren't cheap), it was expecting a little better decor and ambience, and maybe a bathroom that's not through the kitchen. Like all the way. In the room where they wash the dishes.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Great, fresh sea food with a view to boot! Whenever I've been here it happens to be on a weekend and of course with the expected wait. Worth it every time. I could go for some of their clam chowder right now.

Categories: Diners, Salad, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: Iconic last holdout of former large national restaurant chain. Come for breakfast, prepare for a wait. Sunday 10 Am wait was only 1O-15 mins though. Splurge on the fresh squeezed OJ. Try the California Benedict. Tasty Coffee, Great breakfast overall! Will be back for sure !

Categories: Food, Breweries | Review: Best spot in the funk zone. Rad scene and cool bartenders especially Cory and Lyle. Everyone is always having a good time. Great beer as well. Also when you open a tab they give your credit card right back instead of holding on to it. This is fantastic for someone like me who leaves his credit cards at bars every weekend. Thanks so much.

Categories: Salad, Restaurants, Pizza, Sandwiches, Italian | Review: This review is completely about the service because the food is actually 5 stars. I have been here a few times, and every time we encountered rude people. Today for example the man that took our food to our table was extremely rude, when he asked us if we needed anything else and we said we need napkins and a fork he rolled his eyes and leaves gets the stuff comes back and basically slams it on the table and asks us in such a rude way "is there anything else you need??" 
Last time we just got our food to go because again we had a rude woman helping us. 
Again the food is amazing but they need to train their employees better or get new ones that actually like their jobs.

Categories: Steakhouses, Bars, Brewpubs, Breweries, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Sports Bars, Restaurants, Food, Burgers, Comfort Food, Seafood | Review: Totally good local spot. Great beer selection. Calamari was a little soggy but everything else was great. We had a burger and a prime rib sandwich. Both did the job. The staff was the best part. Super sweet ladies!

Categories: Breweries, Restaurants, Sports Bars, Nightlife, American (Traditional), Food, Bars | Review: Had the fish & chips at 8:00 PM. Edible, but certainly not memorable. Looked like the cooking oil was old & cold. My wife's salmon salad was mediocre as well, cold and unremarkable. It also seemed to take much longer than it should for food of this quality to make it out of the kitchen.

Categories: Nightlife, Sports Bars, Bars | Review: A nice departure from the college students and pop music you find in most bars of downtown Santa Barbara. Think upscale dive bar (yes, I realize that is an oxymoron) with a friendly local crowd. Music is selected via Jukebox, a rarity in Santa Barbara.
What I like most is that instead of featuring baseball and football (American), they show rugby and soccer on their flatscreen tv's. When World Cup is on, you can count on them showing it and a packed bar.

Categories: Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Burgers, Fish & Chips, Sandwiches | Review: Wound up here after driving the PCH all day.. so glad we did. Food was amazing and the service was top notch. Jonathan was especially welcoming to us. Small town vibes with a big city menu. Lobster Mac was stock full of lobster tail. Better than I've ever had. Bacon wrapped figs, Boston clam chowder and some flourless chocolate cake were all stellar eats as well. Def recommend this to others traveling to or through Carpentaria!!

Categories: Restaurants, Bakeries, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Coffee Roasteries, Coffee & Tea | Review: I think the coffee here is not only delicious, but it is super strong. It is nice to get a strong cup of coffee that keeps you on your toes all the way until 5p.m. I recommend the red eye, very strong. Very friendly customer service, Axxess card discount, lots of seating for work/studying and wifi! Love this local roasting company!

Categories: Vegetarian, Gluten-Free, Food, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, American (New), Vegan, Cafes | Review: The food was good, at a good price.  The staff are very friendly.  However, this was not the place for us.  It wasn't clear from yelp or the website that this is just a tiny little place with only 3 tables.  We were with our 3 year old and 18 month old and there were no high chairs... so the kids had to sit on our laps and we were barely able to eat our food.   It took a really long time to get our food, and I was confused about the order in which the food was coming out... a few people who were behind us actually got their food first.  
Also, apparently the tables are also considered community tables because some lady with no personal boundaries came and sat at our four top right next to my husband and talked to us the entire meal lol.   I would've given this place four stars, but I really think it should be made much more clear in the website to not take kids here.   If you're not going to have high chairs, you have to advertise that, because as a parent it is a total nightmare to be surprised by something like that.  
I guess I was just disappointed because it was not at all what I expected based off of what I saw on yelp when I picked the place out. Also, I have no idea why yelp says this place is "good for groups."   The place could probably seat 12 people TOTAL (not a group of 12) if you're lucky.   Yelp seriously needs to revise its specifications for this restaurant.

Categories: Diners, Salad, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: We've had poor service and cold food on our visits, all at a steep price for what you get. They seem to live on the waterfront tourist trade, with little draw for the locals. I was hoping it would satisfy my nostalgic craving for the Sambo's that I loved visiting around the country in my youth, but no such luck. Disappointing.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Karaoke, Beer Bar, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Just went here and I am extremely disappointed. First of all, I asked about the Carne Asada fries and I was told that they come with "fries, CHEESE and steak". That's fine, sounds normal. I decided I would rather get the Carne Asada tacos and I asked what comes on them, and I was told that it only comes with the meat. I asked if I could get lettuce and cheese and the cashier responded "lettuce is at the salsa bar and we do not have CHEESE". You do not have CHEESE? FOR TACOS? When I was told literally 10 seconds prior that they serve cheese on the fries??? I do not understand. So I proceeded with my order anyways, and I got my tacos and headed to the salsa bar. The lettuce is not lettuce, it is actually parsley MIXED with onions (???). They do not have lettuce by itself. Lastly, my receipt says I was charged $0.15 for "1 Sorry". I have no idea what a "Sorry" is, nor did I purchase one. I asked what this meant and apparently it is the sales tax, but on the receipt the sales tax was listed separately at $0.47. This place is a complete joke and might as well close down until they can get the ingredients necessary for tacos, as this is a taco place right? Terrible.

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches, American (Traditional) | Review: Came here for breakfast and it hit the spot. The country friend steak was so good and the french toast was yummy as well. The staff so nice check this place out if you are looking for so good simple breakfast.

Categories: Food, Vegetarian, Salad, Restaurants, Cafes, American (Traditional), Burgers, Juice Bars & Smoothies | Review: Natural Cafe is a great place for vegetarians or those who are looking for a meal on the healthier side. 

The new location in the Camino Real marketplace is right next to Hollister Brewing. You order at the counter and take a seat inside (it's not an overly large seating area) or outside on the patio, where they have tables with umbrellas and heat lamps for the evenings. 

The menu of appetizers, salads, burgers, pizzas, pastas, etc. has something for pretty much everyone. I like the Yogi Special (sautéed tofu, brown rice, ample side salad with your choice of dressing that comes on the side) and the Zen Burger (veggie patty burger which comes with chips and salsa) also looked yummy. Friends ordered salad entrees and they were enormous! They don't skimp on the portions here. 

I will definitely be back when I'm in the mood for a casual, healthy lunch or dinner in Goleta.

Categories: American (New), Food, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Why on earth would you ever want to eat here for breakfast? My girlfriend and I went there for breakfast and the woman who took our order couldn't of been any more friendlier - yes, I'm  being very sarcastic. We ordered our food at the window and she was acting as if she had more important things to do than serve us. Apparently customers aren't important here. I ordered the chicken, gravy and biscuits - and potatoes and bacon - which our side dishes came out first. The best thing on the menu was the bacon . Even getting silverware was a hassle. The only person who was decent to us was a smiling woman who was cleaning up afterbus and she promptly provided us a box. Honestly, I ate there an hour ago and I feel gross and sluggish. Is this SB restaurant trying too hard to be like LA? Nice try but even similar restaurants in LA have better service and items on their menu. Definitely not worth it!

Categories: Tuscan, Seafood, Wine Bars, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife, Italian | Review: We were just in the area and stopped in for some dinner. We saw that this place had great reviews so it must be good. Oh my... everything we ordered was so on point! One of the best Italian places I've been to. The Shrimp, clams, & mussels in the spicy red sauce was prob the one thing I would say you must try! The red sauce was so thick and flavorful. We also ordered the salmon carpaccio with arugula. It was delicious as well. My clams with the white wine was soooooo good! & my fiancé's papperdelle alle bosciaola was also another on point item! Overall this place won over my mouth and tummy! The only thing I didn't care for was the bread. I wished they had different kinds. The one they served was too dry and kinda burnt. Def would come back if I am ever in the area again!

Categories: Restaurants, Fast Food, Mexican, Tacos, Burgers, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: SAVE YOUR SELF THE TRIP HERE BECUASE THEY SUCK! 

I lived in a few towns here locally and have trieds several different jack in the cracks, they are my favorite but this one should even be called Jacks. It actully made me change my prefference to Carls jr. I love the tacos but dont go here for them unless you want to be grossed out. Ever time I think service here might have gotten better I always end up dissapointed. I love jacks tacos, just not from this jack in the box. to top if dont complain to those that work their unless you want to be looked at funny or have a worker try and defend themselves like your attacking them. Im just letting you know no need to get all defensive, just fix the issue.

I decided to complained to corporate about there refired tacos and how thier service was horrible. it has happened one to many times, im just fed up with them. The tacos stale and dark brown, not sure if its the oil or they just over cooking them or what ever the issue is but they are not suppose to be like that. icing on the cake, they even forgot to put my chicken sandwhich in the bag and my jalepeno poppers. ok, my mistake for not checking the bag, but that shouldnt be my job. I should get discounted price if i need to check the bag thats like half the work.

When I called corporate they sent me one coupon for my item two weeks later. I was like wow great im going to go back for my combo meal that I order, nope i was mistaken. the manager said it was one only for one item WTF MATE i paid 7 buicks for a spicy chiken combo and you give me a voucher for an item that wont cost over $3.50?. How to they expect to keep my business if they only give me a voucer for one item when my whole meal was ruined becuase I had to go back and forth to pick up the items they forget. gas is not cheap in SB and thats taking time out of my day for a lame inconvience.  

Oh and another thing NAT FLIES by the drinking area is gross. I think the only reason why this location stays open is becuase the college kids dont care, they go in drunk on a late night and keep this shameful bussiness going... Im writing a formal compliant to the heath inspector, lets see if that changes anything. New management or workers? Yes, Please either will be fine!!!!!

Categories: Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Burgers, Fish & Chips, Sandwiches | Review: Loved the service here.   Came in minutes after closing but Jonathan took care of us.   The service, the margaritas, and the tacos (and then dessert) were delicious.     Thanks, guys!

Categories: Food, Italian, Pizza, Fast Food, Gelato, Chicken Wings, Sandwiches, Salad, Restaurants | Review: SUCKS FOR CELIACS!
I love pizza and as a celiac (who CAN NOT have ANY gluten) have been thrilled that more and more places are offering gluten free pizza. Although they offer a gluten free dough at Persona, none of their practices reflect any awareness of cross contamination. They place the pizza in the oven on the same stone as all other pizzas even though there are trays available inches away. Then when I asked the black bearded douchebag operating the pizza oven if they take gluten free precautions he assured me "Oh yes, I thoroughly clean the oven...it's my job blah blah blah" As I watch him using the same oven surface without a tray, same tools everything and thoroughly contaminate what could have been gluten free pizza. To top it off it appeared that some follow up comment from BBD seemed to amuse the staff as I walked out. Wonder if it was gluten related. I get that a pizza place is not the safest place for a celiac and it may be a pain to accommodate. So maybe just don't even offer gluten free dough or like Dominoes use a major disclaimer that you can't guarantee it to be allergy safe or learn decent practices. Also, according to those who ate it (who are used to eating gluten free with me) the dough sucked anyway, chewy and rubbery. Guess I'll be keeping loyal to Pizza Rev from now on and suggest other celiacs do the same.
*Wondering how visible this review will be since I know poor reviews can get buried, for a price.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican, Fast Food | Review: I just left the restaurant, and I have nothing positive to say about my experience. There was only one employee that was behind the counter, helping customers. He looked overwhelmed and not very happy at all. It's understandable, because he was the only employee helping customers. It looked like he may have been new, cause he was having a really hard time wrapping the burrito. During this time, the line was long and I was waiting for a cashier. He had to call twice for a cashier. When the girl came out, she clearly didn't want to be bothered. 
Every single open table was dirty, and food was on the ground. I asked one of the employees nicely if she could clean off my table. I told her that all of the tables were dirty. She didn't say anything. When she was wiping off my table, I said "thank you" twice. She didn't respond. She just walked off. 
Also, a customer ahead of me asked if he could get a little more chicken. An employee said that it would be an additional charge. The customer was given so little chicken! Very poor customer service at this location. You would think that they would be keeping the dining area especially clean because of Covid. They are NOT practicing food service safety! They should be reported to the health department.

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Nightlife | Review: This is our Family's "Go to" spot for breakfast!  The food is excellent...equally important, the service is outstanding, prompt, and attentive. 

My Mom and I decided to try it for dinner last night and I'm sorry to say that it was the exact opposite   We were ignored,  and after getting our water and waiting 10 minutes, I had to get up and ask for a waitress!  Instead of serving our wine while we waited for our meal to be prepared,  I had to again get up, find our waitress,  and ask for our drinks.   

Bottom line;

Good Food
Horrible Service 
Very Disappointing

Categories: Restaurants, Burgers, Sandwiches, Pizza, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: On a road trip with family and was looking for breakfast near Santa Barbara. We went to Home Plate and had a great meal- French toast, huevos rancheros and a egg and pancake bacon breakfast. We were able to sit outside of restaurant - they have some tables spaced out in front. We were able to enjoy our hot meal. The staff checked on us a few times and were pleased with everything. We would definitely go back and happy to support this small restaurant.

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Nightlife, Bars, American (New), Cocktail Bars | Review: I recently went here to have dinner alone at the bar and I was very happy I did! The service is phenomenal and the drinks are delicious! I ordered the chicken sandwich which I would highly recommend with spicy aoli sauce. The atmosphere is lounge vibes meets classic american style and everyone who sat around me was so friendly. They also have their own parking lot so there is no worrying about space like other parts of Montecito.

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), American (New), Southern | Review: Oh Em Gee.  Freakin' amazing!!  We came for brekkie here and was totally 100% overly satisfied.  The service was excellent, no wait, great table.  Fresh OJ and Lemonade right away.  Waitress recommended the Crab cake + poached egg + hash and it was honestly one of the best breakfast dishes I've ever had.  Hubby got the bacon scramble with CORN BREAD, which was out of this world.  I would love to have tried more, but we were so stuffed.  Would definitely go back!!

Categories: Food, Breakfast & Brunch, Bakeries, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea | Review: Was really excited for this place. Extremely limited menu. The food was good but not good enough to justify the prices. The waitstaff was extremely kind to me though.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Diners, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: Good breakfast and lunch in an intimate, cute environment. Make sure you're in no hurry when you come here though. Service is friendly but rather slow. 

On this trip I noticed a couple updates to the menu. They now offer several gluten-free specials and a small selection of local beer.

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Lemon grass salmon. Delicious. Very comfortable with great service. We stop here the 3-4 times we come to Santa Barbara. We look forward to it each time.

Categories: Tapas/Small Plates, Desserts, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Seafood | Review: We had oysters on a half shell, clam chowder, salmon, sea bass, calamari... ALL tasted great. Good food, good wine, great company and very attentive waiters! Comin back!!

Categories: Tacos, Desserts, Nightlife, Bars, Indian, Mexican, Restaurants, Food, Beer Bar, Wine Bars, Fast Food | Review: I had goa taco at Smorgasburg LA and I really enjoyed it. However, this place is far superior. To begin with, its a sit down restaurant so you don't have to wait and eat in the scorching sun for your food. Also, they have more variety than the Smorgasburg LA one and the prices are cheaper and service is faster. Thumbs up!

Categories: Restaurants, Food, American (New), Wine Bars, Nightlife, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Event Planning & Services | Review: Jane is so good! It's my boyfriend's favorite restaurant so we go for pretty much every special occasion. Every server we've had has always been friendly and professional. The food always comes to the table in a timely manner and is hot. The upstairs seating area is so cute and the chicken piccata is the best I've ever had. 

Definitely better to have reservations than to be a walk in as they've been busy every time we've gone.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I had lunch here the other day. Note to self: Control is critical when it comes to the chips here. They are good, and it's easy to fill up on chips alone. 
The tri-tip burrito was great. While it wasn't huge, it was good...which is fine with me...I do not believe quantity is quality.
The server was attentive and prompt. Was it the best Mexican meal on earth? No. Would I eat here again? Yes, and look forward to doing so.

Categories: Restaurants, Chicken Wings, Sandwiches, Breakfast & Brunch, Pizza, Burgers | Review: We really want to like this place. We have been here about 5-6 times and the food is really good. Our troubles have been with the inconsistency in quality of service. A couple times I waited north of an hour for a carry-out order, which was incorrect once we got it. Other times we were unable to even connect with them on the phone to place an order. We also had a bad experience with the food being poorly prepared...just really sloppy and only a few items of each ingredient...very inconsistent with what we had before. If this place can get their customer service in order, we will probably keep coming back, because the food is really good.
Categories: Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, American (New), Bars, Restaurants | Review: In a nutshell: great cocktails, adventurous  and fun menu. Our "go-to" place with out-of-town guests.

Categories: American (Traditional), Bakeries, Restaurants, Italian, Breakfast & Brunch, Food | Review: My wife and I often go here. Last night the Potato Cheddar Pure was wonderful. The food here tastes good. Good value, We often split and go for dessert.

Categories: American (New), Steakhouses, Tacos, Mexican, Seafood, Restaurants | Review: Terrific food, thoughtfully prepared, in an attractive and welcoming setting. Modest, but interesting and reasonably priced, wine (and beer) list. Truly excellent service. Highly recommended.

Categories: Pubs, Bars, Food, American (Traditional), American (New), Nightlife, Breweries, Brewpubs, Restaurants | Review: Came here for the first time last night, everything was delicious! Jamie and jake took great care of us! Will definitely be back!

Categories: Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Diners | Review: Always a pleasure to eat here.  The service is amazing and one manager in particular, I think her name is Miranda, always comes by our table to see how we are doing.  My wife and I have grown to love our visits there and the feeling of family we get.  Thank you!

Categories: Restaurants, Nightlife, Mexican, Bars, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Absolutely delicious best margaritas in Carpinteria great Tex-Mex Mexican food. Be ready for large portions and tons of cheese. I love this place.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food, Bagels, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: Congratulations Jack, you are famous!   

I had a few minutes to nip in and grab a bagel and shmear so I went to Jack's   My son had burned 9 gigs of my iPhone data the first six days of the new month so I am always on the hunt for Wi-Fi to keep my bill under a grand!    No Wi-Fi? thanks Jack!   I am always surprised by places like this with no internet!   They told me it keeps college kids away.   Is that their goal?  To keep people away?  Oh well, it will keep me away, others too.   

Since I couldn't use my phone I figured I would just watch golf or whatever was on the TV.  Oh it was an infomercial!  About acne.   Ewww eating my bagel watching pimple faced teenagers on the TV.   Great subject for a food place.   Maybe that is designed to keep people away too.  

So if you want to make Jack happy, don't go there!   Seems to be their what they are striving for.

Categories: Hawaiian, Food, Sushi Bars, Poke, Seafood, Restaurants | Review: I can't help it but compare PokiRito to Pokemee.  I was more impressed by the quality of the fish here.  The yellowtail was fantastic, it was silky and buttery, without too much bloodline.  The fish were cut to bigger chunks than Pokemee.  

A large bowl is $14, consists of 4 scoops of proteins, 3 scoops of sides, and any toppings your heart desires.  The spicy tuna counts as a side and not a protein, so you get even more sushi grade fish for the price.  There is also no extra charge for tamago and avocado, and bonus point for having kimchi.  The Tom Yum sauce was very flavorful, but I was hesitant to marinate it with the raw fish.  I would much prefer a simple ponzu (not offered here) with the raw fish and then having a rich mayo based sauce on the side for some extra kicks.  My critique would be to add ponzu sauce and get some sturdier bowls.  A large poke bowl here can get quite hefty and the workers were having some trouble picking up the flimsy bowl without spilling the contents.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: We had a meal and drinks in the bar downstairs around 3pm on a Sunday a few weeks ago. It was a quiet day so we sat at the bar outside and had a view of the marina. The clam chowder was super yummy and my favorite. The baked clams and fish and chips were good but nothing I feel I would fun back for. Service was good and friendly. Note: you're not allowed to have your dog on the patio even though it is outside. We didn't have one but a few people wanted to sit outside but the server had to tell them no. I guess something about a city ordinance? So if you have a dog, you have to tie them up to the side.

Categories: Breweries, Restaurants, Sports Bars, Nightlife, American (Traditional), Food, Bars | Review: The food here is amazingly and reliably good. Tonight we didn't want to wait for a table so we went to the lounge. It's the same menu but in an adjacent ex-pool hall they stick the people they can't seat in the main restaurant. 

Horrible ambiance--loud and annoying and, well, everyone in there could have been my child (I'm 41.5) and not the studious sort of 20-year-old either. (Upon rereading, I realize this is awkwardly put; even Michelle Bachmann, if you count her foster children, had 27 kids and there were probably 50 people so not all of them could have been my offspring but any one of them certainly could have been.) 

But our server was terrific and overtaxed, responsible for seating and taking orders. When we arrived, the music was loud but tolerable. Within 45 seconds, the volume shot up and I explained that I had a horrible headache and didn't feel great as I had my period and asked if there was any way they could have mercy on my hormonal head, assuring her that we would not be here long. 

J says this would not have worked on a male server but in my experience men are such weaklings about these matters, I think he would have been embarrassed enough to turn it down a bit for me (and I look wan and exhausted, believably in distress). 

She was great and the music went back to a tolerable level for a bar. Some promotional booze girls in short shorts and super high wedge heels were passing out samples of whiskey, I take it, as their tank tops said Jack Daniels. 

I tried a new sandwich and ADORED it: citrus chicken. It is marinated in beer and something else and comes on a spectacular bun with avocado and pepper jack. I subbed goat cheese and with the red onions and lettuce this was a perfect and relatively healthy sandwich as I made it into an open-faced sandwich. 

The Caesar is mediocre but you get a choice of sides and you can order a side of vegetables if you're trying to be virtuous. 

The chili is always excellent. I dislike wings but my boyfriend likes them on occasion and his were as usual great. 

The burgers are very good as well and the ahi salad is a favorite even of out of towners. 

I'm not into beer and thought my boyfriend's beer far too strong but I'm sure if you like beer you'll like it here. 

There are ten or so starters and pizzas, quite innovative. 

It's just consistently good food at very good prices. 

The Lounge is to be avoided for any mature, civilized adult. But in a pinch if you're hungry and cranky and just want food without a wait, the Lounge won't kill you.

Note: It may indicate something about the clientele that when you sit down for dinner in one of the tables in this ex-pool hall with 11 TVs and a few pool tables, you have to give your server a credit card to open a tab. This is abnormal and suggests to me that the patrons of this establishment are less than trustworthy, grown-up, responsible people. That's fine and I suppose at 20, not everyone is a grown-up, but I've never in my life been asked to give a credit card when ordering a drink at a table.
Categories: Mexican, Tacos, Restaurants | Review: For simple mexican food it is delicous and a great value. To the point, no frills. I will continue to go back for more!

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Caterers, Sandwiches, Bars, Steakhouses, Delis, Nightlife, Burgers, Cafes, American (New), Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: I ordered the Petite Filet Mignon. Was very disappointed when it arrived because it was smaller than the diameter of a soup can, and less than 1/2" thick. It also was over cooked and dry as a bone. Keep in mind, it was $21.95. I jokingly referred to it as "my piece of shoe leather" throughout dinner. Joking aside, I am disappointed that Jill's has become like most most other restaurants in SB: overpriced for the quality and quantity. This used to be my go-to steak place, and in my 10+ years of patronizing Jill's, never did the petite filet come in such a small portion size. Any upstanding steakhouse shouldn't even serve a portion that small. 

Other disappointments include the low quality wine served during happy hour and the overlooked rice pilaf, which had clumps of stuck together rice balls in it where they seemingly scraped out the bottom of the pan.

Categories: Chinese, Food, Dim Sum, Desserts, Coffee & Tea, Restaurants | Review: We were excited to go back to one of our old stomping grounds for lunch. We got there at 2:50 after calling to make sure they served lunch till 3. They told us they were done serving lunch, even though they had people seated. We will not go back there again.

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Caterers, Mexican, Salad, Event Planning & Services, Desserts, Food, Nightlife | Review: Came here when passing through town. For $30 we got a cheese and steak quesadilla and 2 rajas tamales, plus 2 fountain drinks. Quesadilla fed my two kids. I had two tamales and ordered salad instead of rice and beans. Tamales were small and skimpy on cheese. I was still hungry and i eat small meals. The salad was ceasar which croutons which is unusual for mexican restaurant. I have gluten allergy but didn't send it back. My daughter ended up eating it. Salaa bar lookes great and pico de gallo was fresh. Probably not a repeat place for us.

Categories: Pop-Up Restaurants, Korean, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: lunch service was spectacular. cozy and modern atmosphere. big fan of the kpop ambience. 

they were quick to offer to place several tables together for my big family. grand selection of unique soft and hard drinks but my sisters sadly went with Diet Coke. 

presentation is absolutely Instagram worthy. the abundance of toppings add layers of tanginess and crisp to juxtapose the fluffy baos. the owners told us they make the baos fresh everyday and I appreciate the quality. 

Felicia, one of the owners, introduced us to the date cake and milk tea flan and I must recommend both. Date cake is perfect for those preferring gentle sweetness balanced with the creamy cold McConnell vanilla ice cream while milk tea flan is perfect amount of sugar to rejuvenate for any growing child and has some crispy balls. These desserts you cannot taste anywhere else!

Categories: Tuscan, Seafood, Wine Bars, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife, Italian | Review: Went for lunch yesterday and it was excellent.  I've been to Italy twice.  I consider myself a food snob.  And this was as good as Italian food gets.

We ordered the Paparadelle Bolognese and the Pollo Milanese.

Both dishes were terrific.  Great flavor, great presentation, and the right sized portions.

Every time I am in Santa Barbara, we will eat there.  The staff was great and the service was perfect.

I've seen negative reviews and I cannot speak to those.  However, when you make a reservation at 8 PM on a Saturday, it is an ESTIMATE that your table will be ready then.  It is not a guarantee.  Keep that in mind.

When you are accustomed to chain restaurants who fall all over themselves to make even the biggest jerk happy, don't expect the same thing from a place that is independent and doesn't really need your attitude or business.

Categories: Delis, Gluten-Free, Fast Food, Salad, Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: Nice little deli near shopping district.  Seating inside or outside but limited.  Had a Pickles and Swiss Sandwich.  Wife had Veggie Sandwich.  Great!

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Diners, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: AWFUL SERVICE. my mother, my 2 pound chihuahua, and I decide to stop by worker bee for breakfast, outside is cute enough as is the inside. however, the seats are cracking open and rock hard, just plain unsanitary. after being seated, we waited almost 15 minutes for a server to come to our table. both of us understood, however it was not busy whatsoever. anyways, we put in our drink order and soon the waitress comes back. meanwhile, my chihuahua is sitting on my lap. the waitress snatches my coffee out of my hand and asks if the dog is a service dog....... no, he's not. there is not ONE poster or sticker stating no dogs are allowed. and a two pound chihuahua puppy...... seriously??!!?!? we were asked to leave, which we gladly did. awful service, lost our business forever.

Categories: Mexican, Tacos, Restaurants | Review: I'm so glad we stumbled upon this on our way back to our hotel after a long day of shopping. This place had some delicious authentic Mexican food. I got three tacos. I got the chorizo, tongue, and beef. My favorite was the tongue. The meat was super tender and soft. Then, I loved the beef. My least favorite was the chorizo because it was slightly dry. I loved how they had a topping bar. You could add cilantro, onions, radishes, pickled carrots and different sauces on your tacos. The pickled carrots were so good and literally all the sauces were so flavorful.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Restaurants, Cafes | Review: Really wanted to love this place.. We were greeted by really rude and unhelpful workers. epitome of a california accent, but you could at least do a customer service voice to sound like you enjoy your job. my latte was a$$ and the avocado toast was sub par. i got a vanilla latte and it tasted like instant coffee that was brewed in the same room as a bottle or vanilla extract. the bread was a little soggy and the avocado did not seem fresh. you're  better of at dunkin' donuts. lighthouse is so much better and worthy of your money.

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Celebrated Manny's bday at this restaurant last night. The service was great and the food was amazing.

Categories: Korean, Restaurants, American (New), Mexican, Tacos, Nightlife, Fast Food, Bars | Review: Excellent tacos. I got four tacos, and with their Triple Play deal, you can get three tacos for only $9, which is a great deal, considering some tacos are $6 (I think $6 for Ahi Tuna tacos are still a great deal). They're not skimpy on the fillings, and they taste great. 
They are also super fast!

The man at the register is sweet and very nice. 

Also, they must have great customer service because I saw someone complain that they got the wrong taco, and they re-made it quickly. Not only did they re-make the taco for them, but also someone from the kitchen came out, apologized and handed the tacos personally despite their usual "[name] your order is ready" announcement.

Categories: Modern European, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, Bakeries, Wine Bars, Food, Scandinavian, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: We happened upon this place when searching for a place to have a brief business lunch at about 11:00 a.m. on a Tuesday morning.  Some other places were still closed, but we ended up here.  We were informed that they typically didn't serve lunch until around noon, but we were all craving some lunch.  We asked nicely and they went out of their way to talk to the chefs and brought us the lunch menus so we could have our wish.  I was very impressed with the service.

The food was great as well.  We ordered various sandwiches, such as the French Dip and the burger, that were juicy and succulent.  They briefly forgot to bring the juices, or "au jus," for the French Dip sandwich, but it wasn't a big deal.  The portions were perfect, I felt comfortable and satisfied after finishing the meal.  This is a great place with a great location for anyone looking to enjoy a nice meal out on the town.

Categories: Tapas/Small Plates, Desserts, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Seafood | Review: Well we were disappointed. My fish was ok. Not the grand experience we had our first time. My wife's seafood pasta was ok. The appetizers were not anything to write about. I would say it was rather 'blah' of an experience.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Good spot to go for relatively quick, good, casual Mexican food. 

Location/Setting: 
I have to say that the location is kind of unfortunate because  its basically on its own in regards to other restaurants or shopping or anything, having said that, it's nice because it's not typically crowded with tourists. The setting is casual and you stand in a short line to order your food and then take a number and sit wherever you'd like. It's the type of place you could meet with friends for a quick lunch or even do a casual family gathering. 

Food:
There menu is of a decent size and they are also very generous with their servings. It's your typical Mexican restaurant menu that doesn't stray to far from the norm. What does stand out about this place is that they have a really fun salsa bar with dozens of different salsas that you are free to help yourself to accompanied by bottomless chips. Entrees are just above average but very satisfying. If you want to avoid having anything to unhealthy they have a good selections of salads with chicken and avocado and really good dressing that always fill me up!

Service: 
The service is pretty basic but the staff is still friendly and attentive; I've never had any issues. The food typically comes out really quickly which is super nice because I would be the person to  eat 3 gallons of chips if I had to wait too long.

Categories: Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Event Planning & Services, Bars, American (New) | Review: I came here solo for lunch around 1:00. I was seated immediately in the bar area downstairs. A man took my drink order right away. He also noticed my table was a little wobbly so he brought a wedge right over and fixed it. My server came by within minutes as well. I had the fish tacos with crispy cod. OMG it was delicious. The tortilla chips and black beans it came with were also superb. They also being 3 different bottled sauces. Since I'm a wimp I had the verde sauce on the tacos and used it for a dip. It was sooo good. Perfect place for lunch and the service was top notch!

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: I left this place still hungry and extremely disappointed as I had heard good things about the food.... Where is the food? Menu is very sparse and so are the servings. The waiter suggested we order the "sampler platter", which we did. We enjoyed the "meat" on the platter and asked if we could have that as a regular "entrée" item..after asking the chef? .. The waiter said we could not have any more of the meat as there was not enough?  "Not enough" summed up my experience! We ordered 2 bottles of hard cider and were brought one bottle and told they were out! Strange place .. And they could use some help with their interior design/ ambiance. Sorry folks, I just don't get the attraction.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: Food's great.  Creative and spot on.  But the trendy (and tired)  "shared plates" style and menu leads to confusion and over-ordering.  Even though our waiter told it intend "a lot of food" we went ahead.  Our bad.  But a better explanation and menu is called for.  Also, we sat just inside the restaurant and on this December night both front doors were flung open and cold air breezes in.  I get it - COVID restrictions.  Still annoying.  Inside was chaotic and loud - lots of folks I am sure think this is the mark of a winning place, but it was clearly a decibel attack and a challenge to communicate.  But on the things that really matter:  loved the food, had a bunch of leftover and a reasonable corkage - I'll give it 4 stars.  But clearly I can't say this is a "must do".  Other SB spots rival the food with a better experience (for me).

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Chicken Shop, Chicken Wings, Fast Food, Caterers, Event Planning & Services | Review: I love the spicy chicken sandwich.  The crosscut fries are good too but are mostly used to get more delicious Chick-fil-A sauce in my mouth.

The place is always clean with great service.

I haven't been to impressed with the dessert options though.  The cookie sundae was recommended to us but the ice cream was more ice than cream and the cookie chunks were a little hard to chew on.

Categories: Modern European, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, Bakeries, Wine Bars, Food, Scandinavian, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Came here because the wait was out the door for Tupelo Junction Cafe. Andersen's is a delightful spot to people watch. Brunch options were limited, but quite tasty. They have a large staff that is always ready to help - every server is available to any table at any time. They're clearly a well oiled machine with a happy staff.

I look forward to coming back here to give their dessert selection a try.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Lounges, Cocktail Bars, Mediterranean, Bars, Tapas/Small Plates, American (New), Nightlife | Review: Visited here twice while in Santa Barbara. We tried quite a few small and shared plates as well as the rib eye. Everything was presented beautifully and the staff were all excellent.

Highly recommend the crudo.

Categories: Bars, Nightlife, Pubs, Comedy Clubs | Review: Poor service.  Checked my statement the next week and was overcharged.  Bartender added a little extra "tip" for himself.  Sad when they assume everyone is too drunk to remember and use that to validate stealing from good people.  Never going back.

Categories: Restaurants, Chinese, Nightlife, Burgers, Pizza, Asian Fusion, Ramen, Cocktail Bars, Dim Sum, Bars, Pubs, American (Traditional), Japanese | Review: At Last, Santa Barbara has a wonderfully new restaurant that truly offers extremely delicious Ramen.  This is coming from someone that lived in New York City for six years and grew up in The Washington D.C. Area and has been to the top Ramen Shops in both cities often.  I moved to Santa Barbara in 2003 and quickly realized that Ramen would not be a weekly dining option as it was when I lived in the East Coast.  My love for a delicious bowl of Ramen was put on hiatus as I explored my new city and enjoyed what this city had to offer.  I adapted quickly and found my new dining staples, The Shellfish Company, The Santa Barbara Chicken Ranch, Arrigato, The Boathouse, and City Kitchen(I miss you so much!).  Fast forward to March 2016, and I now have Yankee Noodle in my top 6 must support restaurants.  Let's start with Yankee Noodle's service.  When you come to Santa Barbara from restaurant rich cities like Manhattan and D.C. you have to realize that you are simply not going to get consistently good service that's offered in these cities, simply because Santa Barbara can't sustain service industry professionals.  Therefore, the restaurants mostly hire college students.  That being said and having adjusted my grading of service accordingly, I was impressed with the staff of the newly opened restaurant.  They all were very friendly, made me feel welcome, and offered great suggestions on drinks and dining options. Anyone who knows me will tell you that I ask a lot of questions. I had a really nice conversation with the Bartendar, Mirch, about the high quality Beer, Sake, and Cocktail offerings on the menu. Mitch the Bartender came to Yankee Noodle from The Goodland in Goleta.  I started with the "Lemon in the Grass" cocktail.  It's Lemongrass infused Vodka with Ginger and Kumquat syrup. It was the perfect start to my dining experience and gave me something to savor while I examined the very unique menu.  For my first dish, I choose the "Umami Fresh Corn off the Cobb".   I enjoyed every spoonful of the r.  Next I had the "Rich Savory Smokey Pork Broth".   The Broth is hearty, rich, smokey and buttery delicious.  The noodles were authentic and perfectly prepared in soft and chewy Al-dente style with big egg flavor.  You can definitely taste the complexity of flavors from the broth that takes 24 hours to make.  The toppings it came with were excellent.  The Chasu (Pork Belly) was prepared nicely. It was an ample portion that I greatly enjoyed and the cost $12.95 was typical of what you'd find in a top Ramen shop in NYC or D.C.  I took home leftover Ramen and it was just as enjoyable heated up on the stove the next day.  Yankee Noodle occupies the former Space of Union Ale.  They did a nice job on the conversion.  They fashioned a community table from a "Chop Suey" sign that was from a Santa Barbara Restaurant on State Street in 1949's.  It's a great lunch spot while enjoying the Funk Zone or dinner spot Apres Wine with friends or solo.

Categories: Bakeries, Cafes, Coffee & Tea, Food, Desserts, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: I stopped by for cupcakes earlier today. I took 2 bites of my delicious coconut cupcake. As I was taking the third bite I saw a long blonde hair inside my cupcake. It was a disappointment because I didn't want to go back to exchange my cupcake so I just threw it out along with the other two.

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Gastropubs, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Staff from Friday July 16th was incredible! So friendly, so attentive, super friendly and efficient! We love going there for the food and the drinks and the staff really just makes the experience so much better!

Categories: Seafood, American (Traditional), Burgers, Bars, Nightlife, Restaurants, Cafes, Sports Bars | Review: In my opinion preferable to Brophy Bros. No complaints from me! Drinks are fantastic, food is good and service is great! Live music, the harbor, indoor/outdoor seating, just a few of the things making this the perfect destination for a chill but fun dinner out. 
ADDED BONUS I just found out this restaurant will be one of the few to pioneer the way to no straws and eliminating one use plastic waste. Take the time, look into the cause and statistics of plastic straw use. It is such a small sacrifice for such a huge impact. 
Help make our oceans a cleaner healthier place.

Categories: Specialty Food, Cheese Shops, Cafes, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Wine Bars, Sandwiches, Food, Event Planning & Services, Caterers, Bars, Nightlife | Review: The original C'est Cheese is great. They have a small, but great selection of items and I really like the white truffle oil they carry.

The new location looks nice (and after a 1+ years of construction it better), but the selection of food leaves a lot to be desired. They don't offer a lot of items to choose from for lunch and when I go out for lunch I want to be able choose from a wide range of food.  Here you are lucky to see a handful of items. - The new cafe is a let down for me and I won't be back any time soon..

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Delis, Breakfast & Brunch, Tapas/Small Plates, Cafes | Review: This is still the best restaurant in Santa Barbara!! During this past year we have brought over 80 guests here and EVERYONE has agreed with this description! Never had even a mediocre meal! We never take a chance... Always State&Fig

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: I would've given it a 5 star if their servings looked as big as the pictures, especially for the popcorn crawfish. But anyway..
Ambience: 5/5
Service: 5/5
Flavor: 4/5, it could use some more soul and love
Would come again?: YESYESYES! I'm willing to give it another go, maybe their other dishes will be better!

Categories: Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Gluten-Free, Food, Sandwiches, Bagels | Review: Blows all "spare-bucks" and "coffee beany babies" away. Laid back unpretentious, delicious.  Personal.

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: We liked this place the first two times we visited it. However, if three's the charm, it was not in this case. The waiter, Justin, was rude and aloof. Perhaps his girl dumped him the day we were there! Not sure...
When eating at a restaurant of this supposed caliber, one would expect a much better waiter with a much better attitude. The food was OK, but not as good as the first two times, and the menu is limited. 
Because the last experience we had there was so negative, we will not be going back, especially because we were there for a special occasion which was certainly diminished due to the poor service and lack of care given to the whole experience. 
Save your money and find another Santa Barbara restaurant!!

Categories: Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Event Planning & Services, Bars, American (New) | Review: Don't order the scallops with risotto unless you like being ripped off. Only $29.50 for 3 medium scallops over a boatload of risotto. Tastes good, just a really chintzy portion!

Categories: Nightlife, Food, Coffee & Tea, Coffeeshops, Internet Cafes | Review: Seriously the best place ever. Awesome employees, wonderful beverages, delicious food. It doesn't get much better! And it seems to be a nice, quiet, cozy place for studying or doing any kind of work. 
Love this place!

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Diners, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: I am a local and miss Jamie & Oscar and the bee decor, but the new owners are doing a great job with their new menu additions and friendly service. The Worker Bee remains our choice over Esau's and Cajun in food, atmosphere, friendliness.  We are looking forward to the upcoming new additions, like the patio. Thank you for keeping the Worker Bee going.

Categories: Dive Bars, Bars, Lounges, Nightlife, Pool Halls, Sports Bars | Review: It's a dive, but the cheapest drinks in town!  I wandered in there with a couple friends, had a few rounds and we had to question the bartender that our tab was correct.  Impressive.

Categories: Lounges, American (New), Burgers, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Sports Bars, Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: Great neighborhood bar with fun bartenders. They welcomed us with open arms, even if we weren't locals. Highly recommend stopping by for a few drinks and some fun.

Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: The title don't lie. This place has good coffee. You wouldn't go into a place that's called "Mediocre Cup" or "Crappy Cup," wouldja? That's how we felt, and therefore felt safe going into Good Cup as opposed to the Starbucks across the street.

Latte = solid. Not too milky, strong, and served extra hot. Red Eye = coffee + espresso shot, Pamela D. enjoyed. Lotsa couches, friendly staff, newspapers strewn about, random trinkets for sale like cutesy day calendars and "Satan sticky notes" (yea, I'm not sure what those were about either). 

Also, we agreed that it's important to travel with someone who matches your same appreciation (i.e. addiction) for coffee. Non-addicted folks just don't understand.

Categories: Arts & Entertainment, Jazz & Blues, Lounges, Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: Great little bar, tucked away, look for the bak door! 

Lovely patio, inside design is fantastic it's like stepping back in time. Had a few drinks at the bar before heading back to our hotel. 

Highly recommend this special spot.

Categories: Bars, Mexican, Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: HAIR IN 3 SEPERATE  PLATES! Food is great gone like ten times but the new young cooks hair ended up in my chips a side plate of peppers and a salad so just look at your plates good.

Categories: American (New), Food, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Bakeries, Coffee & Tea | Review: Stumbled on this walk up order and take a seat breakfast/bakery. Perfect portions - we shared the California omelette and Belgium waffle plates. Seating outside is nice and the place isn't too noisy. The restaurant was full but we were served within 10 minutes. Great breakfast!

Categories: Pizza, Italian, Fast Food, Restaurants | Review: We came in around 9:30pm and there was just a small family dining in. Walked to the counter and stood there patiently while the young man working kept glancing at us and didn't say a word. After a couple minutes we told him we were ready to order and he made a fuss about having to come over and take our order. We ordered a small pepperoni and pineapple pizza and it came out to almost 38 bucks. Canceled that and ordered 3 slices instead and that came out to 27 bucks. The pizza wasn't bad but there was absolutely nothing special that could explain the high cost of it. I really don't understand how some people can say that this is the best pizza they've ever had.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: What a wonderful restaurant, we loved the decor and the use of repurposed materials. The staff was incredible, we had Norman waiting on us and he is great, made sure to check on our food allergies and a wealth of information on the dishes and drinks. The menu is full of great options and I'm sure we'll come back to try the dishes we didn't get to try. Thank you for such a beautiful dining experience and helping us celebrate our anniversary

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: Exquisitely delicious. Impeccable service and a most delightful cuisine.  An experience worth exploring. Cheers.

Categories: Korean, Restaurants, American (New), Mexican, Tacos, Nightlife, Fast Food, Bars | Review: Our favorite Santa Barbara tacos. We love the triple play-three tacos of your choice for $9. We love the fish taco, bahn mi and crispy taco combination. 

The ahi taco is out of this world too. Tough taco decisions, but so good!

The people taking the orders are quite  unfriendly, but thankfully you don't have to deal with them much.

Categories: American (New), Tapas/Small Plates, Event Planning & Services, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants, Burgers | Review: I thought that this was just oKay. I see why people like it with all the craft beers and the outside atmosphere, but it's a lot of money for ordering at a counter and having your food come out all at different times. The place didn't flow and the food was okay for the exception of the Mexican Street corn which  was awesome and a must try but it didn't come out till my husband and and son were already done with their food and it's supposed to be a starter.

Categories: Steakhouses, Cafes, American (Traditional), Seafood, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Sandwiches | Review: Great place to get a traditional breakfast.  My eggs, bacon, hash browns and toast were cooked perfectly.  The service was fast and friendly and the coffee was strong.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Lounges, Nightlife, Bars | Review: This is the spot for post-wine-weekend wind-down cocktails. Adore the idea of free punch on arrival, and both the cocktail menu and the drinks themselves are lovingly crafted. Best vesper ever.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Bars, Nightlife, Seafood, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Desserts, Food | Review: GREAT FOOD, FRIENDLY SERVICE
I can't begin to list all that makes this restaurant worthwhile, but I will try:

The staff are friendly and love being there! They are "like family" as was explained
They REALLY care that you are happy and enjoying your meal
They are very specific in making your food/coffee EXACTLY how you like it- special requests are honored Mitchell made me the best dry double shot cappuccino ever!
I stayed for three days and ate exclusively there The halibut and cous cous was excellent, followed by baked chocolate cake with vanilla gelato and white chocolate crumble, amazing berry herbal tea, yum.
Day two, Risotto indescribleably good!!
Day three, Roasted chicken with pearl onions red bliss potatoes and long beans , breakfasts were organic eggs fresh baked bread fresh berries maple smoked bacon and the best hash browns I've had, yogurt with granola and berries, and cast iron baked eggs with spinach and potatoes (like a quiche) very good!

getting hungry?
Head over to OUTPOST, see you there!

Categories: American (New), Food, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Walked to this place from our airbnb house. It was a wonderful suprise to see gluten free bread on the menu. My wife had In your face eggs, while I had the gluten free frenchie. Then we topp3d it off with fresh fruit and mimosas. Everything was perfectly cooked and delivered to our table fast. Can't wait to come back tomorrow.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, American (New), Food | Review: Stopped in this morning because I really needed to answer a few emails while traveling. Had a gluten-free pancake and Whalebird kombucha (my favorite). Staff was super friendly.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: Right along Santa Barbara Beach, this place is a perfect spot to sit outside along the sand, get some breakfast and a cup'o'joe, and just relax. The real SB experience here.

The service here is fast and very friendly. The food here may be simple, but it is done well! Just good stuff in a great place. Good prices too.

Categories: Garage Door Services, Restaurants, Live/Raw Food, Food, Home Services, Mediterranean, Keys & Locksmiths, Burgers, Vegetarian, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Vegan, Salad | Review: Mesa Verde is hands down one of our favorite go-to spots for fresh delicious vegan fare.  Not only is their food locally sourced and delicious, but the staff is beyond welcoming and helpful.  Some of my favorites on the menu: 

The Grilled Flatbread - the caper berries are CLUTCH.  
The Cashew Grilled Cheese Sandwich is BOMB.  
Polenta Fries - YUM
Baklava - Just trust me...

I'm one of those annoying people who constantly re-order the menu items they know and love, so bear with me as we make our way through the menu!

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Fabulous every time. Last night in SB had to have Brophy clam chowder. It's so meaty and filling. Great service awesome food party loud lunch atmosphere we didn't mind sitting inside. 
We will return.

Categories: Cuban, Restaurants, Bars, Sandwiches, Nightlife | Review: I stumbled upon this place while I was having a drink at Shaker Mill. They are located in the same building but are two seperate businesses! I've only had cuban food a few times but have never had it in Santa Barbara, so I'm excited to have a new cuisine in town! They are open currently from 4pm-1am so they are open late night which is awesome! From 10pm-1am they have a more limited late night menu but they still have a cuban sandwich and the Plato Mixto on this menu with other sides as well!

I looked over the menu and had no idea what to get. Everything looked so good on the menu it was hard to decide! I ended up going with the Cubano sandwich since I was told that was the most popular. My friend got the Plato Mixto with shrimp and we got a side of the Yucca Fries to share. After we ordered we grabbed our silverware and then headed to a table. They brought the food to us which was super nice!

Our food came very quickly even though they seemed fairly busy. The Cubano sandwich was delicious!!! It was fresh and tasty! The ham and cheese were so good together I loved the sandwich and mustard aioli added a nice kick to it. The Plato Mixto with shrimp that my friend got was huge! It came with yucca fries, a salad, rice and beans. She asked for no beans because she didn't want beans but typically it comes with beans. The Yucca Fries were my favorite part!! They were cooked perfectly soft but sweet and had this creamy aioli to go with them. 

This place was so delicious I love it! I can't wait to come back here soon to try other things on their menu. Once they open for lunch I know I will be coming here all the time. I'm excited to have a Cuban restaurant now on state street! :)

Categories: Salad, Sandwiches, Restaurants, Pizza | Review: Definitely the worst pizza ordering experience I've had.

First, when I called, I was on hold for probably 5 minutes, if not longer. When they finally picked up I told them I would like a personal pan pepperoni. I wanted the personal because I was staying at a hotel and didn't feel like having leftovers. They told me that my order was less than their delivery charge, and would I like to add anything to the order? I said no, that I'll just pay the delivery charge. Then the guy, rather rudely, told me that I couldn't do that, and that I must add something else to the order. Most pizza places allow you to do this, but apparently this one doesn't. So I reluctantly changed my order to a small pizza.

When it arrived I was discovered they had gotten my order wrong. I had received a thin crust pizza instead of the pan crust which I ordered. The pizza tasted like cardboard with sauce and cheese on it. So disappointed.

Categories: Cafes, Restaurants, Sandwiches, Chicken Shop, Salad, American (New), Burgers, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Got the Ahi Tuna Salad and the Earth Burger! Soo good! The salad dressing and salad was my favorite. The burger was flavorful too but the patty was a but too squishy for my liking. But the food is good price for a filling meal. Also the proceeds go back to the community which is amazing

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: Dinner here was such a great experience! The service was amazing. You could tell our server wanted to really make our experience perfect. 
The food was delicious as well! We enjoyed everything we ate. We ordered the French onion soup, pork belly, wagyu beef, and scallop trio. All of them were really tasty.
I loved that the chef delivered our entree and asked us if we had any questions. 
All around good experience.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: I've eaten here three times (spread out since last November til this week.) It's been consistently excellent in both service and food as well atmosphere. 

What I liked about the place aside from the food is that it's just as good of a place to go for a first date as it is for an anniversary or a work dinner.  When you stop to think about it, few places in town are honestly really that versatile in terms of atmosphere. 

The food is amazing. So much to choose from (though, I'm afraid my vegan and vegetarian friends have a much shorter list to choose from) and each choice seems like perfection. The wine list is comprehensive and reasonably priced, all things considered. 

The service is what service should be, in my book. They're super friendly, not overbearing and they KNOW their own menu so they can make good suggestions if you're at a loss or overwhelmed by choices! 

Bottom line: A rare restaurant in SB that is actually really, really good all around. Good for tourists and locals alike with great food every.single.time.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: I can't rave enough about how great this place is! We started with the deviled eggs - so good! Even had a piece of bacon sticking out of them. Brussel sprouts were to die for - such a great flavor. Next came the heirloom tomato salad. Everything was so fresh! Finished off with the risotto. Fresh peas, grilled fennel. My only regret was not saving room for dessert!!

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), American (New), Southern | Review: 4.5 Stars

I had the chicken wrap.  It was very good with very fresh veggies.  The chips were also tasty.  The lemonade is homemade....sooo good!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Best fresh homemade salsa on Linden in Carp. Lunch and dinners always are good. Authentic, long time local Mexican food, sixteen years.

Funky old school, nice local family. Small and cozy. 
There are always lunch and dinner specials on the sign in front. Check it out. 

Local home made flavors, no worries here. 

Yumster.

Categories: Nightlife, Restaurants, Steakhouses, Bars, American (Traditional) | Review: Eating at High Sierra (at sea level) is like eating at a Denny's with a bar.  Outside of restaurant has no landscaping and the interior is horrible. It looks like amateur decorator failed attempt.  We sat at a booth where half of the table was in glaring 80's track lighting and the other half of the table was in TOTAL darkness!  Really?!  We asked why was the lighting was so bad ? And we needed a different table.  The waitress was perplexed and had to ask a manager for help.  The food was terrible.  Ribs were obviously smoked on premises, yay! But hopelessly dry and over cooked, BBQ sauce too sweet.  Chili was terrible, as was the side salad and garlic bread.  Bacon cheeseburger was good but not special.  Tastes like they are cutting a lot of corners and not cheap as it should be.  Too bad for Goleta.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Breweries, American (Traditional), Brewpubs | Review: Although the food was decent and the beers weren't terrible, I still can't get over how indifferent the staff is.  The hostess almost acted overtly pissed that I said I was going to sit at the bar (I was dining by myself).  The bartenders, although not as pissy, were very pretentious, consistently making snide comments about different patrons to each other.  Most likely I will steer clear.

If you do make your way in,  the mussels appetizer was very good, and the meatloaf was better than most.


Edit Note--  a new to me bartender(female) helped me finish out the night and was heads and tails better than the others.  She was very knowledgable, friendly, and professional.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: I was quite disappointed with the food. My husband and I came here for a romantic dinner. The location is stunning. Right next to the beach and views are amazing. I ordered a blood orange margarita and it didn't taste right. I asked them to put some additional triple spec in it and when they brought it back it tasted like water. I didn't even drink half of it. I ordered the ahi tuna with wonton chips off the happy hour menu. The tuna was good but the wonton chips were not crispy at all! Tasted stale. I asked them about it and the waiter said that they are room temperature and that's how they come! We then ordered a brownie sundae. How could you get this wrong? Brown was crunchy and not fudgey and ice cream tasted more like ice milk. My husband got the steak and he said it was ok. I know I won't go back here. Service was a bit slow too.

Categories: Food, Breakfast & Brunch, Bakeries, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea | Review: The best brunch place in Santa Barbara! If you are in the area, you HAVE TO try this place!

I don't even know where to begin. Every thing on the menu tastes sooo good. So if anything on the menu sounds appealing to you, chances are it will taste amazing. 

Recommendations: 
-THE KAHULA BANANA FRENCH TOAST: every bite melts in your mouth, it's so soft and fresh... I wish I could eat this every day haha. 
-Any of the egg benedicts! They come with potatoes and a small bowl of mixed fruits.

Categories: Specialty Food, Health Markets, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Bakeries, Coffee & Tea, Desserts, Breakfast & Brunch, Food | Review: Wow!!!  Stumbled upon this cute bakery while shopping.  Having Celiac is difficult and it's even more difficult to find something sweet and savory that fits my dietary needs.   I got loaves of bread to go and will likely regret not buying more.  While there, I got 1/2 a grilled cheese with turkey and a slice of cake.  My friend got half a Turkey sandwich with pumpkin soup.  She also got a piece of lemon cheesecake to go.  All of it was amazing!!!

Categories: Arts & Entertainment, Food, Bars, Nightlife, Wine Bars, Wineries, Wine Tasting Room | Review: We were suggested by Martin Brown @ Kalyra to pop in here and were glad we did.  (When we originally passed it, we didn't even realize it was a tasting room at the time.)  Apparently, this concept was a combination of different vintners coming together and they have done a great job in what they do.  The wine pourer was friendly and, although it was strangely unpacked (It was a Friday and there were also only a handful of people that entered Kalyra while we were there earlier), we definitely can see this place as a hit.

Bottom Line - Another fun spot to hit when in the "funk zone"/on the urban wine trail. =)

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food, Desserts, Turkish, Restaurants, Bakeries | Review: Avoid unless nothing else is open and you need a dessert to address a cravings emergency . 

Growing up I ran into three types of halwais (dessert makers) - 1. Delightful folks with great product, 
2. People with bad attitude but outstanding product (think Soup Nazi from Seinfeld), and,
3. Staff with lousy attitude and a product you can get elsewhere. 

Lokum falls in the last category. If you are craving for a dessert late in the evening, you could go there as they are open late. The lokums and baklava weren't too bad but the pistachio in lokum was bit rancid.

I should add some context about why I felt unwelcome by the staff/owner.  I had entered the shop from the rear entrance after asking a staff/worker if that was ok. Once they said fine, I just walked up to the counter without realizing that the alley led me to the wrong side of the counter given the shop's layout. I was dressed down by the grumpy young bearded guy for the mistake. He didn't care to apologize after I explained that it wasn't an intentional invasion of his counter space ‍ . We just moved on to "business" .  Then he was miffed again at enquiry about taking a warmed kunefei. I realize that it can be an extra step to transfer it from the pan to a box but I have taken Kunefei to go at restaurants before.  The guy seemed to act as if the process in his shop is universal. 

Anyway if their product was similar to Karolay Gulluoglay, I might have considered going back.  But, it's not worth spoiling my day by visiting these guys who have a product you can get at non fancy bakeries.

Categories: Tapas/Small Plates, Restaurants, Comfort Food | Review: Yummy apps all fried up and ready to be devoured.
